title: prefers-color-scheme
description: A media feature that responds to the user's system dark/light mode preference. The same component can adapt to both modes in pure CSS — no JavaScript theme switching needed. Widely available since 2020.
html:
Adaptive Card

This card responds to your system's color scheme preference. Try toggling dark mode in your OS or browser settings — or use DevTools (Rendering tab → Emulate prefers-color-scheme).
challenge: The card has light styles by default. Add a @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) block that gives .card a dark background (#1e293b) and light text (#e2e8f0) when the system is in dark mode.
realWorldUsage: The standard approach for supporting dark mode in any CSS codebase — applied at the component level so each element adapts independently, or at :root level to swap CSS custom properties that cascade everywhere. Modern design systems define light/dark token values in @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) blocks rather than managing a class toggle.
